item.title: Return of the Meme: 1486 (sparklesparrow)
item.description:
Let's get this bread. Biscuits are baked, like bread. Running low on bread memes again.
Probably should have saved this for a Wizardpost but eh.
Sparklesparrow.
No, but i have had coffee sourced from Laos and it was pretty damn good.
The stakeout yesterday was a bust, as far as lost Arctic gulls and terns go. I didn't have even One Good Tern, let alone another. But the Eared Grebes, Cassin's Vireos, and White-crowned Sparrows had arrived for the winter practically overnight.
Honestly, that would be better than any stripper story I might have.
This is what the Protestant Revolution has gotten us: the debasing of Ska Day. Along with Christofacism.
This is an obscure theology joke.
I'm probably a cryptid. Or a flock of birds.
They are also immune to damage from spices.
Really, this is the boss of any southwestern tourist town in Arizona or New Mexico.
This happened in the First Punic War. He got angry at the omens given by the sacred chickens so he threw them overboard and lost the battle the next day to the Carthaginian Fleet.
As Lemondrop says, "unmake me."
Today has been decided that it's cleaning day. The stars have aligned.
And yet when I open up it feels like sometimes I am told I need to get over my past. My people, i'm only able to open up about the darkness in my history because I have done the work to get over it. If you don't want **the lore**, don't ask.
Pippin is a Coelurosaur nibbling on my ear right now.
Eat a billionaire.
Accurate
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: "oh. Pippin. Pippin. What the fuck. Pippin. Pippin." ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself. 3) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on. 4) all funds from the sales of items at these two links will be sent to support charities aiding to fight for Trans rights in the US (not just T-shirts!) https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74812801-all-conures-are-anarchists https://www.teepublic.com/t-shirt/74811778-whiptail-pride 5) Since multiple people have requested this over the last two weeks or so, my ko-fi is: https://ko-fi.com/codyandpippin
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Walking today. With weights on hands and ankles. Also wearing weighted vest.
item.description: So yesterday was a bit different because I also had 1 pound weights on my hands. It was difficult in the beginning because I wasn't use to it. Had to catch my balance and get re-centered. A few weeks back I hurt my knee and its taken around a month to get it feeling better. I hope everyone is doing well and having a great day.
item.dc:creator: kaosnole95

item.title: That's all he needed to hear

item.title: ICE dipshit doing dipshit things
item.description: Most viral edit: thanks for the upvotes. Side note: I’ve been reading about recruitment in the Nazi era and how they faced challenges finding good soldiers. For some reason, the job attracted dirtbags, violent criminals, and failed or wannabe cops. What an odd coincidence that ICE also faces similar hiring challenges. I’m sure it’s just a fluke
item.dc:creator: DownvotesStarWars

item.title: Pixie & Brutus: Invincibility - Part 3
item.description: Today's Tiabeanie Tax; Tia in her Tutu. Tia showing off her adorable Halloween costume. I'm sorry I missed yesterday's post. Luna isn't doing very well and I can't currently get her into the vet. My days have been very busy and very stressful. Still, I missed my post, so as an apology, I've added two new albums to the Kofi of Tiabeanie in her tutu and guinea pig costumes from 2023. https://ko-fi.com/theunnamedpoet All pet pictures are 100% free for everyone to enjoy!
item.dc:creator: TheUnnamedPoet

item.title: Command & Conquer – the brilliant Tiberium introduction
item.description:
On the 26th anniversary of the original game I take the opportunity to praise the creeping horror of the alien crystal, as told through cutscenes (check wifi).
September 26, 1995, was Command & Conquer (later labled Tiberian Dawn) released by Westwood Studios. It is an iconic strategy game with successful spinoffs.
Westwood had formalized the RTS [real-time strategy] genre with Dune II in 1992, where 'spice' is the resource to fund wars. When making their own sci-fi RTS, set in a near future, Westwood invented an extraterrestrial resource: crystalline Tiberium.
The main inspiration was the film The Monolith Monsters (1957): a meteor crashes in California and a chemical reaction makes its fragments drain silicon (even from human bodies which die), while water causes replication and growth. But the processes are ended (spoiler) by a mixture of water and salt.
Tiberian Dawn lets you command GDI, the Global Defense Initiative (gold/kaki), or the terrorist organisation named the Brotherhood of Nod (red/grey). The intro mentions Tiberium and it is soon seen on game maps [highlight in yellow] where it grows out of the ground and form fields, like some shiny, toxic Chernobyl cabbage.
The alien substance, first discovered at the river Tiber in Italy, came to earth with meteorites. Its toxicity is apperent as infantrymen are hurt by contact with it. Still, for the economic trade off, players find it positive that Tiberium multiplies (by itself or through infected trees) so that it can be harvested by vehicles.
The game intro video mentions how Nod is leading in Tiberium extraction and has almost half of the world supply, but GDI is not shy to utilize the alien crystal either. Since it funds the armies players are conditioned to appriciate the valuable resource (promoted by Kane, the Nod leader) to achive the appointed goal: victories on the battlefields as either GDI or Nod.
Yet cutscenes between missions (a mix of CGI and real actors; above GDI general Sheppard) increasingly add news about the alien substance. As a player you focus on your appointed missions, and Westwood were confident in letting the peril grow at the perifery of your attention and out of your control.
I find it fantastic world building, also without the later titles' expanded tiberium lore. Here follow full or parts of concerned cutscenes [with transcriptions]. These are mainly from the GDI campaign as Nod usually have the same content, if sometimes with a voice over that trivializes the risks and promotes Tiberium usefulness. Several scenes are narrated by a computer system known as EVA;
EVA CONCERNING NOD OPERATIONS: These efforts are suspected to be funded by Nod's access to vast Tiberium deposits. Tiberium continues to confound the scientific community, soaking up ground minerals and soil nutrients like a sponge. The end result of this unique bleaching process creates the formation of Tiberium crystals, rich in precious metals and available for collection with the minimum of mining expense.
REPORTER: Exclusive photos smuggled out of the World Tiberium Research Center show evidence of serious ecological consequences in Tiberium rich areas. Whether these unusual effects on plant life have any relationship to recent reports of Tiberium poisoning in humans, remains to be seen.
[infected trees spreading spores]
[a haunting view of a growing infestation]
COLONEL CARTER, POISIONED BY TIBERIUM: Welcome to the big time, Commander. You haven't really arrived until Kane has sicked his media ninja on you at least once. I'm pinch hitting for Sheppard since no one's really sure where he is right now. The wrong time to take a vacation, we're down about 60% manpower from the T-poisoning.
EVA: Attention. Stand by for Tiberium dangers update. Tiberium is named after the Tiber River in Italy where it was first discovered. There are now more than 200 areas of the Earth affected by Tiberium deposits. Tiberium appears to be spreading by means of conveyance unknown. HOST EDDIE: Doctor Mobius, tell our viewers just what Tiberium really is.
DR MOBIUS: Molecularly, it's a non-carbon-based element that appears to have with non-resonating reversible energy, that has a tendency to disrupt carbon-based molecular structures, with inconsequent and unequal positrons orbiting on the first, second, and ninth quark rings! HOST EDDIE: Whoa! Is that good or bad? DR MOBIUS: Well, let me put it this way Eddie, the possibilities of Tiberium... are limitless... EVA: We now know that not only does Tiberium leach elements from the soil, but it appears to also leach vital nutrients from all plant life. Human contact with Tiberium is extremely toxic and often fatal. Exposure should be avoided.
EVA: GDI-funded research shows the following incidents of Tiberium-related illnesses... Respiratory Ailments; Reproductive Ailments; Immune System Ailments; Tiberium Related Deaths. Spores emitted by Tiberium-infected plants appear to be a source of Tiberium-related illnesses. The world scientific community is focusing its research on this deadly spore.
DR MOBIUS: Tiberium is a new lifeform. Quite simply put, it seems to be adapting the Earth's terrain, foliage, and environment to suit its own alien nature. If this is the case, ladies and gentlemen, we are facing a killer beyond that of our most turbulent nightmares. It is not an exaggeration to state that the future of the entire planet may be in jeopardy. May God have mercy on our souls...
Neither a victory as GDI or as Nod offers a solution or an explanation to how mankind will adapt to the resource that has fueled victory but now stands to ruin us. The similarities to fossil fuels feel poignant, even though they are accidental. Westwood would as told expand upon the use, consequences, and origin of the crystal; to the point the original C&C series is called the Tiberium Universe or Tiberium series, to differentiate it from the spinoff series Red Alert and Generals.
That falls outside the scope of this post, but I will end with how in Tiberian Sun, the Tiberian Dawn sequel set about 40 years later, most of Earth is infected and much of modern civilization has crumbled into ruin. Polluted regions are home to mutated flora and fauna and even humans, while vast areas are turned into a toxic, barren hellscape where GDI and Nod face off once more.
item.dc:creator: Ngugi

26 Imgur Creator Spotlight: @TheCrochetedSunflower made this hobbit wreath and it's cool.
27 Man Tax: Wentworth Miller
“Nature is busy creating absolutely unique individuals, whereas culture has invented a single mold to which all must conform. It is grotesque.” - U.G. Krishnamurti I try to keep my posts "good vibe zones". So, instead of finding the one thing in here you don't like and commenting only on that, also comment about something you DID like. Balance the negativity. Love you long time. So, I made a discord server. It's a relaxed environment where you can take/donate memes to the cause, play trivia twice a week, chat about lots of topics or about nothing at all. We're also setting up Jack Box game night and movie night! Very laid back/relaxed vibe. Come hang with us. https://discord.gg/AWFV7FT3RQ https://ko-fi.com/mememuseumcurator Feel free to leave me a tip. Those that have, thank you so much.
item.dc:creator: MemeMuseumCurator

item.title: Ten ToolGifs you may have missed lately.
item.description:
Of a broom.
Repairing a Burst Sewer Pipe with the Freezing Method.
Operating track camera during a relay race.
Two-stroke shovel.
You've never seen signatures like this before.
Laser Weeder.
Field Marking Robot.
Screen printing.
PVC gutter extruder. As always, I learned A LOT watching those vids. And they're amazing ! Enjoy, have fun.
item.dc:creator: TerribleBot
